question_text: A hydraulic system has a small piston with area 2 cm² and a large piston with area 40 cm². If a force of 500 N is applied to the small piston, what force is produced at the large piston? Assume the pressure is transmitted equally throughout the fluid.
explanation: In a hydraulic system, pressure is the same throughout the fluid. The pressure at the small piston is 500 N / 2 cm² = 250 N/cm². At the large piston, this same pressure acts on 40 cm², giving a force of 250 × 40 = 10,000 N. This demonstrates how hydraulics can multiply force.
